text:
User: it should error out if that channel already exists so the agent knows about it [...] `channels create` on an existing channel name is now a hard error instead of a silent dedup. The daemon bails with an actionable message: [...] ```
channel "dup" already exists under this parent (id <id>). Switch into it instead: tenex-edge channels switch dup
``` [...] The error propagates through the RPC layer to the CLI, which prints it and exits non-zero — so the agent knows the channel is already there and is pointed at the `channels switch` command to join it (rather than wrongly assuming it minted a fresh room).
